title: Instrument d'adhésion

pages.text:
(Translation)

•

Pyongyang, February 15 Juche 90(2001)

Your Excellency,

I have the honour to advise you that the Democratic People' s Republic of Korea has decided to become a member of the Office International des

Epizooties.

The Government of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea has examined the provisions of the International Agreement and of the Internal

Statutes of 25 January 1924 and agrees to participate in the working costs of the

Office under the conditions stated, especially with regard to Article 3 of the

International Agreement and Article 11 of the Internal Statutes.

The 6th 'contribution category has been selected by my Government:

The official Delegate of the Democratic People' s Republic of Korea to the OIE will be Dr. Pak Jong Gwang, Director of the Veterinary and Antiepidemic Department, Ministry of Agriculture.

Yours sincerely,

Paek Nam Sun

Minister

Ministry of Foreign Affairs

D.P.R.of Korea

-

Mr. Hubert Vedrine

The Right Honourable Minister of

Foreign Affairs of the Republic of France

Paris

Copy to: Director General, OIE

DELEGATION PERMANENTE

DE LA REPUBLIQUE POPULAIRE DEMOCRATIQUE

DE COREE AUPRES DE L'UNESCO

MS 2.36, 1 rue Miollis, 75015 Paris

Tél. : 01 45 68 25 64

Fax: 01 45 68 25 63

No. 016/DL/01

The Permanent Delegation of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea to

UNESCO presents its compliments to Office International Des Epizooties (OIE) and has the honour to forward a letter of membership request to H.E. Mr. Hubert Vedrine,

Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of France, which is the official depository of the International Agreement by H.E. Paek Nam Sun, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea.

The Permanent Delegation of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea to

UNESCO avails itself of this opportunity to renew to OLE the assurances of its highest consideration.

| ¡

2 12001 (Juche 90)

TO: Office International Des Epizooties 12, Rue de prony 75017 Paris, France

Tel: 0144151888, Fax: 0142670987
